title: aeschynomene pygmaea var. pygmaea
intro: Aeschynomene pygmaea var. pygmaea, also known as dwarf sensitive plant, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been studied for their potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Aeschynomene pygmaea var. pygmaea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
